vault.relationship: Layer 1 — the Vault defines the 'why.' Read it first for design philosophy, principles, anti-patterns, interaction signatures, and voice. Vallejo (this system) is Layer 2 — the 'how.' When they conflict, the Vault wins.

designPrinciples.precision: The warmth of competence. Obsessive alignment, spacing, typographic discipline. When members stake five figures on vehicles they may never have touched, visual sloppiness signals institutional carelessness.
designPrinciples.institutionalConfidence: The quiet authority of a platform that has moved millions of vehicles. We don't hedge, over-explain, or decorate. One primary action per view. Verb-first labels. No exclamation marks. No 'Oops.'
designPrinciples.vitality: Auctions are alive. Bidding is competitive, time-bound, consequential. The design channels that energy — motion confirms action, status is always visible, urgency is real. But vitality never becomes chaos.

questions: Does this read at multiple depths?
designFilter.rule: If the answer to any question is no, the design is wrong.

critical: The blue flood — everything is blue so nothing has emphasis. Blue earns authority through restraint.
Bootstrap-default aesthetics — generic card-with-border, badge-pill, muted-gray signals 'we didn't think about this.'
Tables as default layout — tables are a last resort. Spacing and typography create structure, not borders.
AI-generated defaults — purple accent, border-box foundation, emoji/icon overuse, generic typography, the 'helpful default' that feels machine-generated.
The 'it just needs to function' mentality — craft IS communication. Precision signals trustworthiness.

readOrder: 0. Read the Copart Design Vault (https://github.com/getboyce/Copart-Design-Vault) for design philosophy, principles, and anti-patterns. This is the 'why' — Vallejo is the 'how.' When they conflict, the Vault wins.
1. You are reading this file (SYSTEM-INDEX.json) — the manifest and context primer.
2. Read QUICK-REFERENCE.md for CSS variables, HTML patterns, and component snippets — this is your primary implementation file.
3. Read COMPONENTS.md only when you need detailed specs for a specific component (anatomy, variants, states, accessibility).
4. Reference copart-design-tokens.json only when you need exact hex values, font stacks, or dark mode equivalents.
5. Read ICONS.json when you need to implement icons — it contains SVG path data for all 120 design system icons.
6. Use ../components/tokens.css for CSS variables — import it, never duplicate token definitions.
7. Use ../components/header/ for the Copart header — always include it at the top of every page unless told otherwise.
rules: Always include the Copart header at the top of every prototype page unless told not to. Use components/header/index.html (logged-out) or components/header/loggedin.html (logged-in). Never recreate the header from scratch.
Shared CSS design tokens live in components/tokens.css. Import it rather than duplicating variables.
No pure red anywhere. Use the rose/magenta error palette (error-border: #D42A54).
No emoji in UI. Use Iconic icon set (inline SVG only, stroke="currentColor").
One Primary (blue) button per view — this executes Institutional Confidence. Secondary actions use Secondary or Tertiary variants.
Orange accent is for registration/onboarding CTAs and urgency badges only. Never for primary actions. Orange channels Vitality — genuine urgency, not decoration.
Dark mode: toggle via data-theme="dark" attribute on <html>. Semantic tokens swap automatically.
Monospace font (JetBrains Mono) for VIN numbers, lot numbers, bid amounts, timestamps. Numbers are first-class citizens — see Vault > craft/typography-voice.md.
Always pair color with icon + label for state indication.

UX Writing: sentence case everywhere, active voice, verb-first button labels with no pricing/amounts (actions only), error format is '[what happened] — [how to fix]', use canonical terminology (Lot not listing, Bid not offer, Watchlist not favorites, Member not user, Location not yard, Wholesale not Select, Pre-bid not preliminary bid). See QUICK-REFERENCE.md § UX Writing.
Tables are a last resort. Prefer cards, lists, or stat blocks. Use tables only when comparison across multiple simultaneous dimensions is the primary task. See Vault > craft/density-philosophy.md.
Watch for AI-generated anti-patterns: purple accent, border-box foundations, emoji/icon overuse, generic typography ignoring the type scale. See Vault > language/anti-patterns.md.

QUICK-REFERENCE.md.purpose: CSS variables, HTML patterns, and copy-paste component snippets for every component

COMPONENTS.md.purpose: Detailed component specs — anatomy, variants, states, accessibility requirements
COMPONENTS.md.readWhen: Need detailed specs for a specific component

copart-design-tokens.json.purpose: Machine-readable design tokens — exact hex values, font stacks, dark mode mappings
copart-design-tokens.json.readWhen: Need exact hex values or dark mode mappings

ICONS.json.purpose: SVG path data for all 120 design system icons (Iconic set), organized by category with usage metadata
ICONS.json.readWhen: Building any UI that includes icons

../components/tokens.css.purpose: Shared CSS design tokens — all CSS variables (colors, spacing, radius, shadows, motion, z-index). Single source of truth.
../components/tokens.css.readWhen: Building any UI — import this instead of duplicating CSS variables

../components/header/.purpose: Working header component — header.css (styles, @imports tokens.css), header.js (behavior), index.html (logged-out), loggedin.html (logged-in)
../components/header/.readWhen: Building any page — always include the header unless told not to

brandContext.domain: Online vehicle auctions — salvage, wholesale, clean title marketplace
brandContext.fontPrimary: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

semantic.feedback: For each severity (success, warning, error, info): --{severity}-bg, --{severity}-text, --{severity}-border, --{severity}-icon

feedback: Modal / Dialog (3 sizes + mobile bottom sheet)
dataDisplay: Table (sortable, responsive — last resort, prefer cards/lists)
